title: Two Hands on the Tablet
body:
## Two Hands on the Tablet

Maya wanted to draw a sea turtle. Her brother Lucas wanted to play his racing game. They both reached for the family tablet at the same time.

"I had it first," Maya said.

"You used it yesterday," Lucas said. "It's my turn."

They held the tablet between them. Neither one let go. The tablet beeped because two fingers were pressing the screen at once.

Their mom heard them from the kitchen. She came into the living room with a small notebook in her hand. She set the notebook on the table. The cover had a sea turtle on it. Maya thought that was funny.

"Lucas," Mom said, "how long do you want to play?"

"Twenty minutes," Lucas said.

"Maya, how long do you need to draw?"

"Twenty minutes too," Maya said.

Mom drew a clock face on the notebook. She marked two times. One time was for Lucas. One time was for Maya. She gave the tablet to Lucas first. His racing game would close after twenty minutes anyway. Maya could draw on paper while she waited.

Maya was quiet for a moment. She did not love the plan. But it was a fair plan. She got out her colored pencils. She drew the sea turtle on a piece of notebook paper. The shell looked even better than she had pictured in her head. She drew tiny waves around it. The waves looked like real water.

When Lucas's twenty minutes were up, he handed the tablet to Maya without being asked. He had not heard the timer. He just remembered.

Maya looked at her paper turtle. She looked at the tablet. She decided to keep her drawing on paper.

"You can have my turn," she told Lucas. "I already finished my drawing."

Lucas grinned. He started a new race. Maya pinned her turtle picture above her desk.
